question.text: What is the fundamental difference between a futures contract and an options contract?

answer.text: In futures, both parties have obligations; in options, only the seller has an obligation while the buyer has a right
Futures are traded on exchanges while options are traded over-the-counter
Futures have no expiry date while options expire on a fixed date
Futures require no upfront payment while options require premium payment

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** - Futures: BOTH buyer and seller have OBLIGATIONS to fulfill the contract - Options: Buyer has a RIGHT (can choose), Seller has an OBLIGATION (must honor if exercised)
**The Trap:** Both futures and options are exchange-traded in India. Both have expiry dates. Futures require margin (not "no upfront payment").
**Cross-Exam Context:** This distinction explains why option buyers pay premium but futures buyers pay only margin.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Futures = mutual obligation. Options = right (buyer) + obligation (seller).
